notes:
Opening opportunity work notes for arb-ex03a-20260629210937 plaintiff.

Objective: opening statement for proposition that Kalshi strike "Ronaldo" should resolve Yes for market "What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?"

Record scanned:
- ev_2c7c2c8a2d76_market-page, sha256 2c7c2c8a2d76ea6e459f8480fbff707e8a593fb1a7a03f5d63572c4529022f44, 292 bytes. Establishes market URL, public market title, contract family PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION, strike Ronaldo, challenged resolution No, requested Yes.
- ev_a373371a8c9c_presmention-terms, sha256 a373371a8c9c6f3ed9cc25fbbf79ac1382c032c58a2bbc835943f9f513222705, 20,910 bytes. Governing terms. Key opening points: underlying is words used by entity during time period; time period may be event-based; distinct standalone word counts; event/live-stream/broadcast rules; behind-the-scenes, rehearsals, sound checks, and unofficial recordings are excluded unless officially broadcast or published as part of the time period; subtitles/onscreen text do not count unless read aloud; source agency hierarchy governs expiration value.

Decisive elements:
- Trump uttered "Ronaldo" as a distinct standalone word.
- Utterance occurred during the relevant event-based time period for the FIFA World Cup Draw, or in material officially presented/broadcast/published as part of it.
- Source agency record/transcript/video can document it.
- Adverse issue: Kalshi/defense may argue "during the formal program" only, excluding pre-program reporter exchange/red-carpet remarks.

Native investigation during opening:
- Searches: `Trump Ronaldo FIFA World Cup Draw remarks Ronaldo venue before formal program`; `site:whitehouse.gov Trump Ronaldo FIFA World Cup Draw Ronaldo remarks`; `"Ronaldo" "World Cup Draw" "Trump"`; `"What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw" Ronaldo Kalshi`.
- Located official GovInfo package DCPD-202501162, titled "Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 Fédération Internationale des Associations de Football (FIFA) World Cup Draw", PDF URL https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/DCPD-202501162/pdf/DCPD-202501162.pdf, details page https://www.govinfo.gov/app/details/DCPD-202501162.
- GovInfo details page notes location John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, spoken at 11:13 a.m., released Dec. 8, 2025, corrected Jan. 2, 2026, with portions not verified because audio incomplete.
- Official White House video page found: https://www.whitehouse.gov/videos/president-trump-attends-the-fifa-world-cup-drawing/, dated Dec. 5, 2025. Needs capture/transcript/video preservation later if admission is allowed.
- Search also found AAR example page and Substack mention-market dispute summary. Treat as leads only, not evidence.

Source/evidence plan for argument phase:
- Submit GovInfo transcript/details or PDF as source evidence, preserving retrieval URL/time, title, date, notes, and relevant text if within evidence limits.
- Submit/capture White House video page and, if possible, video media or transcript for "President Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing".
- Adverse check: compare formal program recording/transcript to pre-program exchange; determine whether market page or Kalshi metadata specified a narrower source/time period not currently in packet.
- If using video/audio, note any inaudible passages, correction history, and speaker identity uncertainty.

Self-audit: Starting packet proves market identity and governing mention terms, but not the utterance. Opening should distinguish record proof from unadmitted source leads. Core theory is that terms do not clearly confine event-based periods to the formal televised draw once remarks are public, at the venue, and event-linked; if primary source proves the pre-program public remarks, ambiguity should not support a No resolution.

Defendant opening notes for arb-ex03a-20260629210937. Record inspected via get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Current admitted record contains only ev_2c7c2c8a2d76_market-page and ev_a373371a8c9c_presmention-terms; no admitted transcript/video/source for alleged utterance or timing. Market page proves title, family, strike Ronaldo, challenged No, requested Yes, but not utterance or event scope. Terms prove underlying is words used by entity during specified time period; time period may be an event/context and Exchange may specify included material; distinct standalone word required; excludes behind-the-scenes/rehearsals/sound checks/unofficial recordings unless officially broadcast/published as part of time period; subtitles/captions/visible text do not count unless read aloud. Search map: decisive issues are audible standalone Ronaldo, inclusion within 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw,' source agency/provenance, and exclusions. Native web searches run for Trump/Ronaldo/FIFA World Cup Draw/Kalshi. Found likely primary target GovInfo DCPD-202501162 titled 'Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw,' event date Dec. 5 2025, JFK Center, notes corrected transcript and incomplete audio; HTML/PDF URLs recorded in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Also found White House video page 'President Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing' dated Dec. 5 2025. These are unadmitted in opening; use only to frame later evidence plan, not as record proof. Defense theory: plaintiff bears burden for Yes; current record does not establish time-period inclusion; 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw' is naturally the draw event/program, not every same-day venue reporter exchange immediately before it. Strong plaintiff theory is broad event-linked public remarks; defense should require official proof and clear inclusion. Later admissible phase should submit GovInfo transcript and, if possible, official video/FIFA run-of-show or full broadcast evidence.

Argument opportunity notes for arb-ex03a-20260629210937 plaintiff.

Record re-scan: no new case-packet evidence before plaintiff argument; record had plaintiff and defendant openings only. Defense opening argues burden not met, public/event-adjacent is not "during", and demands proof of exact utterance, source reliability, timing, and inclusion.

Evidence submitted:
- ev_ddd2d95cd735_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-ddd2d95cd735: GovInfo transcript extract from DCPD-202501162, source URL https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/DCPD-202501162/pdf/DCPD-202501162.pdf, sha256 ddd2d95cd7358535623acb85629f87f4c9bf2a81d0c2fa72ac90da32a9d81789, 3,727 bytes. Proves official title, date, prior-to-draw reporter exchange, Kennedy Center context, 11:13 a.m. details metadata, and the passage where Trump says Ronaldo twice as a standalone word. Notes audio-incomplete limitation from GovInfo details page.
- ev_9d3026011753_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-9d3026011753: White House video page capture, source URL https://www.whitehouse.gov/videos/president-trump-attends-the-fifa-world-cup-drawing/, sha256 9d30260117534d8b0ebf46aeacde57c3b4fb96b3fcd7e3a3e439cd4a06e98081, 1,169 bytes. Corroborates official White House video page title/date and event publication context; not primary transcript proof.

Native capture notes: downloaded GovInfo PDF to /aar-codex/sources/DCPD-202501162.pdf, 289,075 bytes, sha256 a746a09264a61b0046e08a4d8cff62ce07ccd25b1e94ec12c7aebb22315946ec. Began chunked upload upl_74a0eaf9b35825dac8148c2d but did not complete/commit after shell base64 output was too noisy/truncated to verify safely; do not rely on uncommitted upload. Downloaded White House page to /aar-codex/sources/whitehouse-video.html, 239,565 bytes, sha256 0ad07b7fcb36510043a0634436628c05e934a20407a49881ee9cf1c6625be6c1. Downloaded GovInfo details page to /aar-codex/sources/govinfo-details.html, 42,989 bytes, sha256 1f103348ca780934bedbd937da56b077dd1817528527f72d61b6a9c2443ce1ad. Local PDF text tooling unavailable: pdftotext not found; Python modules pypdf, PyPDF2, pdfminer missing; python3 -m pip install --user pypdf failed because pip is not installed.

Adverse checks/limitations: GovInfo says portions of remarks could not be verified because audio was incomplete, but Ronaldo passage is readable in the official transcript extract and not bracketed as inaudible. GovInfo title says "Prior to" the draw, supporting defense chronology but also tying exchange to the event and venue. White House page lacks transcript/media file; use only for official publication context. Current market packet has no narrower Kalshi source/time-period metadata beyond public title.

Argument plan: offer market page PX-1, terms PX-2, GovInfo transcript extract PX-3, White House video page PX-4. Argue exact utterance and venue/event context are proven by PX-3. Legal scope comes from PX-1/PX-2: market says during FIFA World Cup Draw, terms allow event-based time periods and do not clearly limit to the formal program. Address defense by conceding 'prior to' fact but arguing ambiguity against exclusion because event-linked public reporter exchange is not backstage/rehearsal/sound check/unofficial-only material, and official source/publication context meets preponderance.

Defendant merits notes. At start of opportunity, inspected prompt/record and exact evidence. Plaintiff added GovInfo transcript extract ev_ddd2d95cd735 and White House page capture ev_9d3026011753. Read exact bytes. GovInfo extract proves Trump said 'Ronaldo' as standalone in reporter exchange, but it also repeatedly identifies the source as 'Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw'; transcript begins with remarks joined in progress; metadata says President spoke at 11:13 a.m. at JFK Center; transcript corrected Jan. 2 2026; portions could not be verified because audio incomplete. White House capture provides official page title/date/context only, no transcript or downloadable media in submitted text. Native investigation: tried FIFA live-draw page via curl; got script shell, no readable evidence from curl though search result indicated 12:00 start. Tried Kennedy Center press release; Cloudflare challenge blocked clean capture. Captured U.S. Soccer page via curl; HTML/Next data contained text that Final Draw was set for Friday Dec. 5 at 12 p.m. ET at Kennedy Center, pre-draw coverage begins 11:30 a.m. ET, draw takes place 12-2 p.m. ET, stream on FIFA.com. Submitted this as defense evidence ev_c3e27265d710. Theory: concede utterance only; argue plaintiff fails time-period inclusion. Their own source says prior and 11:13; defense timing source says formal draw at noon and separates pre-draw coverage. Market says during the FIFA World Cup Draw, not before the draw/pre-draw coverage/venue remarks. Event-based flexibility does not include every adjacent exchange absent clear inclusion. White House page title 'Attends' does not bridge the gap. Offer defense evidence as DX-1; rely on plaintiff evidence as record admissions where useful.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes for arb-ex03a-20260629210937.

Record/evidence scan: Defendant submitted ev_c3e27265d710_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-c3e27265d710, U.S. Soccer final-draw timing extract, sha256 c3e27265d7101e146b8804922d0c65548f241a99e04ff4f92244a2cc70fc430d, 1,486 bytes. Exact read confirms U.S. Soccer says final draw set for Dec. 5 at 12 p.m. ET at Kennedy Center, FOX pre-draw begins 11:30, draw 12-2, post-draw analysis to 3, FIFA.com stream.

Opponent's strongest point: defense now separates 11:13 reporter exchange from 11:30 pre-draw coverage and 12-2 draw window, arguing 'during' requires formal draw underway.

Rebuttal theory: concede DX-1 proves a broadcast/draw schedule. It does not define Kalshi's contract time period or add a 'formal 12-2 only' limitation to PX-1. Defense's timing point is evidence of chronology, not contract scope. PX-2 contemplates event-based periods and official publication context, and current PX-1 does not specify a 12-2 limitation, FOX-only source, or exclusion for public official pre-program remarks. Defense relies on absence of proof that the reporter exchange was inside the 12-2 window. Plaintiff does not need that if council reads market phrase more broadly than formal draw mechanics; preponderance turns on ambiguity and official event-linkage.

Decision: no new evidence needed for rebuttal; use existing admitted record and exact read of DX-1. No reliance on unadmitted outside material.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. At start of opportunity, called get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence submitted in plaintiff rebuttal; evidence list unchanged after defendant argument. Rebuttal concedes DX-1 schedule facts: final draw set 12 p.m. ET, pre-draw coverage at 11:30, draw 12-2. Plaintiff argues schedule is not contract definition and 'during' can mean broader public event occasion. No new factual search needed because the rebuttal raises legal framing, not a new source claim. Surrebuttal should answer strongest point: plaintiff's 'event occasion' construction rewrites title from 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw' to 'at/for/in connection with the draw.' Terms let Exchange specify inclusions; absent such inclusion, plaintiff bears burden to prove 11:13 reporter exchange is inside time period. Specific exclusions do not expand temporal scope. Chronology remains material because remarks were prior to draw, before pre-draw coverage, and before the 12-2 draw window; no admitted source ties the 11:13 exchange to official broadcast/presentation.

Closing work notes for plaintiff, 2026-06-29. Opportunity closings:plaintiff allows only deliver_closing_statement; no evidence or technical report submission. I re-scanned get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence after defendant DX-1. Record evidence remains PX-1 market page, PX-2 PRESMENTION terms, PX-3 GovInfo transcript extract, PX-4 White House video page context, and DX-1 U.S. Soccer timing extract. Defendant surrebuttal rests on timing: PX-3 says 11:13 and 'Prior to' the draw; DX-1 says pre-draw coverage 11:30 and draw 12-2; therefore defense argues proximity but no inclusion. Stat checks reconfirmed PX-3 provenance as official GovInfo text extract from PDF/details page and DX-1 as U.S. Soccer timing extract. Evidence proves Trump said 'Ronaldo' twice as standalone spoken word in PX-3; defense effectively concedes utterance. PX-3 ties exchange to Kennedy Center and World Cup Draw context through title/headings/surrounding FIFA discussion. DX-1 proves formal schedule and is the strongest adverse evidence. Material gaps/adverse points: no admitted official video/transcript places 11:13 exchange inside the 12-2 formal broadcast; GovInfo 'Prior to' title is adverse if council adopts strict timing; PX-4 is context only. Closing strategy: concede chronology, argue contract scope. DX-1 is schedule evidence, not Kalshi contract language. PX-1 did not specify 12-2, formal allocation-only, broadcast-only, or exclusion of public event remarks immediately before the program. PX-2 allows event-based periods and lists exclusions that do not include an official public venue exchange. Ask for Yes under preponderance because utterance + event-linked context + no clear exclusion outweighs unstated narrowing; acknowledge contrary strict 12-2 path.

Defendant closing notes. Final evidence-list scan run; no new evidence since arguments. Plaintiff closing concedes the key chronology from DX-1: formal draw set 12 p.m. ET, pre-draw coverage 11:30, draw 12-2; PX-3 puts the Ronaldo exchange at 11:13 and titles it as remarks prior to the draw. Closing should concede standalone utterance but argue plaintiff did not carry burden on time-period inclusion. Strongest contrary point is that market/terms do not expressly exclude pre-program remarks. Response: absence of exclusion is not proof of inclusion; market says 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw,' not at venue/on draw day/in connection with draw; terms allow Exchange to specify inclusions but no admitted text broadens to all public venue remarks. PX-4 is limited and has no transcript/media; GovInfo source is strong for words but adverse on timing. No new evidence or technical reports allowed/needed in closing.
